# gostartv2

> An opinionated Go backend starter project — layered architecture, JWT auth, sqlc + goose, and strict code-quality tooling.

## Overview

gostartv2 is a reusable foundation for future Go backend projects. It provides a complete, production-leaning skeleton with layered architecture (handlers → services → repositories → models), JWT authentication with rotating refresh tokens, sqlc-generated type-safe database queries, goose SQL migrations, structured logging with slog, and a strict 40+ linter golangci-lint configuration.

## Tech Stack

- Go 1.26
- chi v5 (HTTP router)
- sqlc (type-safe SQL → Go)
- goose (SQL migrations, embedded)
- pgx/v5 via database/sql
- golang-jwt/jwt/v5 (JWT auth)
- bcrypt (password hashing)
- validator/v10 (request validation)
- log/slog (structured logging)
- testcontainers-go (integration tests)

## Project Structure

```
cmd/api/main.go              → entry point, bootstrap, graceful shutdown
cmd/migrate/main.go          → goose migration runner (explicit CLI)
internal/
  config/                    → typed Config struct, env loading + validation
  logging/                   → slog setup (JSON prod, text dev)
  httpx/                     → JSON response/error helpers, error envelope
  middleware/                → Recoverer, RequestID, CORS, Auth, RateLimit, Logger
  database/                  → pgx pool + health check
  db/sqlc/                   → sqlc-generated code (DO NOT EDIT)
  repositories/              → hand-written repos over sqlc queries + tx helpers
  services/                  → business logic layer + sentinel errors
  handlers/                  → HTTP handlers, DTOs, request validation
  auth/                      → JWT sign/verify, refresh tokens, auth errors
  models/                    → domain types
  testutil/                  → testcontainers Postgres helper
db/queries/*.sql             → sqlc query sources
migrations/*.sql             → goose SQL migrations (embedded via go:embed)
```

## API Endpoints

- GET /health — liveness
- GET /ready — DB readiness
- POST /api/v1/auth/login — exchange credentials for tokens
- POST /api/v1/auth/refresh — rotate refresh token
- POST /api/v1/auth/logout — revoke refresh token
- POST /api/v1/auth/logout-all — revoke all sessions (Bearer required)
- POST /api/v1/users — register a new user (public, rate-limited)
- GET /api/v1/users — list users (Bearer, cursor pagination)
- GET /api/v1/users/{id} — get user (Bearer)
- PUT /api/v1/users/{id} — update user (Bearer)
- DELETE /api/v1/users/{id} — delete user (Bearer)

## Key Conventions

- Layered: handlers → services → repositories → models
- Doc comments on all exported symbols
- Sentinel errors with package prefix (e.g. "services: user not found")
- consumer-side interfaces (defined where consumed, not where implemented)
- //go:build integration for DB integration tests
- No os.Getenv outside internal/config
- golangci-lint v2 with 40+ linters

## Environment Variables

18 env vars — see .env.example for the full list with defaults and comments.

## Make Targets

make verify — build + lint + test (full gate)
make test — unit tests with -race
make itest — integration tests (requires Docker)
make lint — golangci-lint
make sqlc-gen — regenerate sqlc code
make migrate-up — apply migrations
